name: claude-code-cache-fix
description: Cache optimization for Claude Code in VS Code. Proxy mode (v3.0+) or preload interceptor. Fixes prompt cache bugs that cause excessive quota burn.

commands.title: Claude Code Cache Fix: Show Status
Claude Code Cache Fix: Enable Proxy Mode (recommended for CC v2.1.113+)
Claude Code Cache Fix: Disable Proxy Mode
Claude Code Cache Fix: Open Proxy Request Log
Claude Code Cache Fix: Test Connection to Anthropic API
Claude Code Cache Fix: Export Windows Corporate Certificates

claude-code-cache-fix.autoStartProxy.markdownDescription:
**When `true` (default):** on activate, the extension silently enables proxy mode — sets `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` in global `claudeCode.environmentVariables` and spawns the local proxy server as a managed child process. No command-palette step needed.

**When `false`:** nothing auto-starts. Use the command palette (`Claude Code Cache Fix: Enable Proxy Mode`) to turn it on manually.

On CC v2.1.113+ (Bun binary) proxy mode is the **only** way to get cache fixes — the preload interceptor doesn't work on Bun. Keep this on unless you have a specific reason to manage it yourself.

claude-code-cache-fix.autoInstallInterce.markdownDescription:
**When `true` (default):** if the `claude-code-cache-fix` npm package is missing on activate, the extension installs it using whichever package manager owns the Node toolchain — `volta install` when `npm` resolves to a Volta shim, otherwise `npm install -g`. Progress surfaced via a VS Code notification.

**When `false`:** a warning popup appears asking you to run the install yourself.

The extension needs the npm package for the proxy server and the preload interceptor; without it, neither mode works.

claude-code-cache-fix.debug.markdownDescription:
**Diagnostic.** Enables verbose debug logging from the preload interceptor to `~/.claude/cache-fix-debug.log`. Includes per-call dumps of prefix hashes, applied/skipped fix counts, and breakpoint traces.

The proxy has its own telemetry in `~/.claude/cache-fix-proxy-log.ndjson` (NDJSON, one line per `/v1/messages` call) — open it via `Claude Code Cache Fix: Open Proxy Request Log`.

Leave off for normal use; turn on when reproducing a bug.

claude-code-cache-fix.prefixDiff.markdownDescription:
**Diagnostic.** Saves system-prompt prefix snapshots to `~/.claude/cache-fix-snapshots/` on every call, so you can `diff` across sessions to find what's changing and busting the cache.

Large output — can fill gigabytes over a long session. Leave off unless actively investigating a cache-instability report.

configuration.title: Claude Code Cache Fix: Corporate environments (proxies, custom CAs)

claude-code-cache-fix.httpsProxy.markdownDescription:
**Corporate HTTP(S) proxy URL** for upstream forwarding to `api.anthropic.com`. Required behind Zscaler / Netskope / Forcepoint / Bluecoat / corporate squid.

Example: `http://proxy.corp.example:8080`

Leave empty to use the system `HTTPS_PROXY` / `HTTP_PROXY` environment variables. When set, this is passed into the proxy child's environment; the proxy itself honors it via `hpagent` (requires `claude-code-cache-fix@>=3.0.3`).

**Verify:** run `Claude Code Cache Fix: Test Connection to Anthropic API` from the command palette. On success the proxy log shows `[upstream] using proxy http://... for https upstream (rejectUnauthorized=true, ca=default)` on the first forwarded request.

claude-code-cache-fix.noProxy.markdownDescription:
Comma-separated host list to bypass the corporate proxy. Matches curl/Python/Go semantics.

- `*` — bypass all
- `.suffix.example` — bypass that suffix and any subdomain
- bare host — bypass exact match and `*.host`

The default covers loopback and `.local` mDNS. Add your corporate domain suffix (e.g. `.corp.example`) to bypass internal services.

claude-code-cache-fix.caFile.markdownDescription:
Path to a PEM file with extra CA certificates, for SSL-inspecting proxies (Zscaler, Netskope, corp MITM).

Leave empty to use the system-default trust store. Alternative to setting the system-wide `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` environment variable — setting it here scopes the CA to the cache-fix proxy only.

On Windows, enable `autoExportCerts` below to have the extension generate this bundle automatically from the Windows certificate store.

claude-code-cache-fix.rejectUnauthorized.markdownDescription:
**Verify TLS certificates on upstream forwarding.** Keep `true` unless you have no other option.

**Setting to `false` is an insecure escape hatch** — disables all cert validation. Use only as a temporary workaround while IT provides the correct corporate CA bundle; re-enable as soon as you can. When disabled, the proxy prints a one-time warning to the output channel on first request.

claude-code-cache-fix.autoExportCerts.markdownDescription:
**Windows only.** On activate, export matching certificates from `Cert:\LocalMachine\Root|CA` and `Cert:\CurrentUser\Root|CA` (subject-pattern matched — see `certSearchPatterns`) to `~/.claude/cache-fix-corp-ca-bundle.pem`. When `caFile` is empty, this path is automatically used.

Useful for Zscaler / Netskope / Forcepoint environments where the CA chain lives in the Windows trust store but Node doesn't read from there.

Idempotent — reruns pick up cert rotations.

claude-code-cache-fix.certSearchPatterns.markdownDescription:
Certificate subject patterns for `autoExportCerts` to match against. PowerShell `-like` wildcards, matched against each cert's Subject field.

Add your corporate CA's distinguishing substring (e.g. `*YourCorp*`) if it isn't one of the default MITM vendors. You can also add multiple if IT uses different CAs for different environments.

configuration.title: Claude Code Cache Fix: Proxy-mode fixes (CC v2.1.113+)

claude-code-cache-fix.skipFingerprint.markdownDescription:
**Fix:** Strips the unstable `cc_version` fingerprint (e.g. `2.1.92.a3f`) from the system prompt before forwarding. Claude Code computes this fingerprint from `messages[0]` content including meta/attachment blocks — when blocks shift position on resume, the fingerprint changes, the system prompt changes, and the entire prefix cache busts.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is skipped.**

**Cost of skipping:** In a validated 7.5-hour Windows session (536 calls), 81% of calls had fingerprint instability — each one a full cache miss (~6-30K tokens of `cache_creation` instead of `cache_read`). Cache hit rate drops from 95-99% to 60-80% on resume sessions.

**When to enable (`true`):** only if upstream CC ships a fingerprint-computation fix and the health status shows `dormant` across multiple sessions.

Applies to both proxy-mode (pipeline extension `fingerprint-strip`, order 100) and preload-mode. See [impact guide](https://github.com/cnighswonger/claude-code-cache-fix/blob/main/docs/extension-impact-guide.md#1-fingerprint-strip-order-100).

claude-code-cache-fix.skipToolSort.markdownDescription:
**Fix:** Sorts tool and MCP-server definitions alphabetically by name before forwarding. Tool definitions can otherwise arrive in different orders between turns; since the API cache key hashes the full request body, different tool ordering produces a different cache key and a full cache miss.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is skipped.**

**Cost of skipping:** On sessions with 3+ MCP servers, tool-order jitter was observed on ~15% of calls. Each jitter event triggers a full prefix rebuild: 150K-400K tokens of `cache_creation` depending on context size.

**When to enable (`true`):** only if CC implements deterministic tool ordering upstream. Sort is idempotent — if tools are already sorted, no modification occurs.

Applies to both proxy-mode (pipeline extension `sort-stabilization`, order 200) and preload-mode. See [impact guide](https://github.com/cnighswonger/claude-code-cache-fix/blob/main/docs/extension-impact-guide.md#2-sort-stabilization-order-200).

claude-code-cache-fix.skipSessionStartNo.markdownDescription:
**Fix:** Applies the same block sorting to the first turn of a fresh session that Claude Code applies to subsequent turns. CC's `normalizeResumeMessages` has an early-return on `length < 2` that skips sorting — so the first call after `/clear` or a new session has unsorted blocks, busting the cache prefix for that turn.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is skipped.**

**Cost of skipping:** First turn of every fresh session gets a full cache miss. Not catastrophic (one miss per session) but expensive on sessions with large system prompts. A validated test showed that with the fix, call 2's `cache_read` equals call 1's `cache_creation` to the exact token; without, call 2 is a full miss.

Applies to both proxy-mode (pipeline extension `fresh-session-sort`, order 250) and preload-mode. See [impact guide](https://github.com/cnighswonger/claude-code-cache-fix/blob/main/docs/extension-impact-guide.md#3-fresh-session-sort-order-250).

claude-code-cache-fix.skipCacheControlNo.markdownDescription:
**Fix:** Pins `cache_control` markers (the `{"type": "ephemeral"}` annotations that tell the API what to cache) at canonical positions — the last block of the last user message. Without normalization, markers can drift between turns, shifting the cache boundary and causing partial cache misses.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is skipped.**

**Cost of skipping:** Marker drift causes partial cache misses on each turn. Not full misses, but enough to increase `cache_creation` steadily over the session.

Applies to both proxy-mode (pipeline extension `cache-control-normalize`, order 400) and preload-mode. See [impact guide](https://github.com/cnighswonger/claude-code-cache-fix/blob/main/docs/extension-impact-guide.md#5-cache-control-normalize-order-400).

claude-code-cache-fix.skipTtl.markdownDescription:
**Fix:** Detects the server's cache TTL tier (1-hour or 5-minute) and injects appropriate `cache_control` ephemeral markers to maximize reuse within that window. On cold starts, the server assigns a 5-minute TTL until the first cached call promotes to 1-hour; TTL management accelerates that promotion.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is skipped.**

**Cost of skipping:** Requests may lack TTL markers entirely, leaving caching to server defaults which may not be optimal. An A/B test on CC v2.1.117 showed the proxy (all extensions on) achieved a 95.5% cache hit rate vs 82.3% direct on the first warm turn — TTL management is a significant contributor to that 13-point gap.

Applies to both proxy-mode (pipeline extension `ttl-management`, order 500) and preload-mode. See [impact guide](https://github.com/cnighswonger/claude-code-cache-fix/blob/main/docs/extension-impact-guide.md#6-ttl-management-order-500).

configuration.title: Claude Code Cache Fix: Preload-mode fixes (CC ≤v2.1.112)

claude-code-cache-fix.skipRelocate.markdownDescription:
> ⚠️ **Preload-mode only** — no effect on CC v2.1.113+ (Bun binary). Use proxy mode for current CC versions.

**Fix:** Scans all user messages for relocated attachment blocks (skills listing, MCP servers, deferred tools, hooks) and moves the latest version of each back to `messages[0]`. On resume, these blocks drift from `messages[0]` to later messages, changing the cache prefix.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is skipped.**

**Cost of skipping:** This is the original bug that started the project. Resume sessions without this fix burn 10-20× more than expected — scattered blocks cause a full prefix rebuild on every turn. See [anthropics/claude-code#34629](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/34629).

See [impact guide](https://github.com/cnighswonger/claude-code-cache-fix/blob/main/docs/extension-impact-guide.md#block-relocation-cache_fix_skip_relocate).

claude-code-cache-fix.skipSmooshSplit.markdownDescription:
> ⚠️ **Preload-mode only.**

**Fix:** Peels `system-reminder` blocks out of tool results so they don't smoosh against the actual tool output in a way that destabilizes the cache boundary.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is skipped.**

**Cost of skipping:** System-reminder content mixed into tool results changes the request body hash per turn, partial cache misses accumulate.

claude-code-cache-fix.skipContinueTraile.markdownDescription:
> ⚠️ **Preload-mode only.**

**Fix:** Strips the `--continue` trailer blocks that CC appends when resuming a conversation — these trailers vary per invocation and break prefix stability.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is skipped.**

**Cost of skipping:** `--continue` sessions lose cache on the continuation boundary.

claude-code-cache-fix.skipDeferredToolsR.markdownDescription:
> ⚠️ **Preload-mode only.**

**Fix:** Restores the `deferred_tools` block when an MCP server reconnects mid-session. Without this fix, MCP reconnects can drop the block entirely, triggering a downstream cache miss.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is skipped.**

claude-code-cache-fix.skipReminderStrip.markdownDescription:
> ⚠️ **Preload-mode only.**

**Fix:** Strips bookkeeping reminders (e.g. todo-list nag inserts, usage warnings, session-state reminders) that CC injects unpredictably. These reminders mutate the request body and destabilize the cache.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is skipped.**

claude-code-cache-fix.skipToolUseInputNo.markdownDescription:
> ⚠️ **Preload-mode only.**

**Fix:** Normalizes `tool_use` input-field formatting (whitespace, key order in JSON-like args) to avoid spurious cache-key changes when the same call is re-issued.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is skipped.**

claude-code-cache-fix.skipCacheControlSt.markdownDescription:
> ⚠️ **Preload-mode only.**

**Fix:** Preserves `cache_control` sticky markers (promoted positions) across turns so the API's cache boundary stays pinned where we want it, rather than drifting back to wherever CC puts them next.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is skipped.**

claude-code-cache-fix.normalizeIdentity.markdownDescription:
> ⚠️ **Preload-mode only.** Proxy mode has `identity-normalization` always on in the pipeline (order 300).

**Fix (opt-in):** Normalizes the subagent identity string in `system[1]` so it's canonical regardless of whether the session was initiated via `Agent()` or `SendMessage()`. Without normalization, switching between these modes changes the system prompt and busts the cache.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is active.**

**Impact:** Agent SDK users mixing `Agent()` and `SendMessage()` get a full system-prompt rebuild on every mode switch when this is off.

See [impact guide](https://github.com/cnighswonger/claude-code-cache-fix/blob/main/docs/extension-impact-guide.md#4-identity-normalization-order-300).

claude-code-cache-fix.normalizeCwd.markdownDescription:
> ⚠️ **Preload-mode only.**

**Fix (opt-in):** Replaces volatile CWD and path references in the system prompt with stable placeholders. Useful for cross-worktree cache reuse — two worktrees of the same repo can share the cache prefix after normalization.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is active.**

**Tradeoff:** can mask genuine path-based ambiguity in the model's reasoning. Enable only when you're actively doing worktree-heavy work and value the cache reuse more than path-context fidelity.

claude-code-cache-fix.normalizeSmoosh.markdownDescription:
> ⚠️ **Preload-mode only.**

**Fix (opt-in):** Normalizes dynamic values inside `system-reminder` blocks — token-usage counters, budget figures, output-token counts, todo-reminder text — so reminders don't mutate on every turn.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is active.**

Aggressive — set only when you've confirmed that system-reminder churn is your cache-miss bottleneck (via `prefixDiff` snapshots).

configuration.title: Claude Code Cache Fix: Prompt & image rewrites

claude-code-cache-fix.imageKeepLast.markdownDescription:
> ⚠️ **Preload-mode only** — no proxy-mode equivalent yet upstream.

**Fix:** Keeps images (read via the `Read` tool, stored as base64) in the last N user messages; replaces older ones with a text placeholder.

**Recommended: `3`.** Enough for multi-turn vision reasoning, strips the compounding tail.

**Cost of `0` (disabled):** A single 500 KB image costs ~62,500 tokens per turn on Opus 4.6, ~85,000+ on Opus 4.7 (35% tokenizer inflation on images). Multiple images compound. In a session with 4 screenshots, disabling image stripping added ~250K tokens per turn — equivalent to doubling the context-window usage.

See [impact guide](https://github.com/cnighswonger/claude-code-cache-fix/blob/main/docs/extension-impact-guide.md#image-stripping-cache_fix_image_keep_lastn).

claude-code-cache-fix.stripGitStatus.markdownDescription:
> ⚠️ **Preload-mode only.** There is a **better native alternative:** set the environment variable `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_GIT_INSTRUCTIONS=1` instead. Same effect, no interceptor needed, works on Bun-binary CC too.

**Fix (opt-in):** Strips CC's injected `git status` output from the system prompt. CC otherwise embeds live git-status on every call; any file edit changes git-status → system prompt changes → entire prefix cache busts.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is active.**

**Impact:** A validated test with the native flag set showed 18-token `cache_creation` across git-state changes (vs thousands without). Saves ~1,800 tokens per call (the removed `git status` block plus related Bash-tool description text).

See [impact guide](https://github.com/cnighswonger/claude-code-cache-fix/blob/main/docs/extension-impact-guide.md#git-status-stripping-cache_fix_strip_git_status).

claude-code-cache-fix.outputEfficiencyRe.markdownDescription:
> ⚠️ **Preload-mode only.** Modifies the model's system prompt.

Replace Claude Code's `# Output efficiency` system-prompt section with custom text. Leave empty to keep the default.

**Not a cache fix** — purely behavioral. Used by operators who want to steer how Claude balances thoroughness vs terseness. Three known variants are catalogued in the [output-efficiency-prompts reference](https://github.com/cnighswonger/claude-code-cache-fix/blob/main/docs/output-efficiency-prompts.md).

**Caution:** changing this mid-session changes the system prompt and **will bust the cache** until turn 2 of a new session.
